title: Thin Template Softening Execution Log — Phase 20.7
principle: Converted thin tool/scenario templates from 'win this SERP independently' into 'calculation result example + full calculator CTA' format.

changes.rationale: Removed aggressive CTA. Thin variant now reads as 'example', not 'personal calculator'.
Removed Q&A hook. Descriptive example label.

changes.before: if (yrs) parts.push(`for ${yrs} Years?`); parts.push("See YOUR Compound Growth");
changes.after: if (yrs) parts.push(`for ${yrs} Years`); parts.push("Compound Growth Example");
changes.rationale: Removed '?' question hook and 'See YOUR' hook.

changes.before: let desc = `What's YOUR monthly payment for a`;
changes.after: let desc = `Monthly payment example for a`;

changes.before: desc += `? Get a personalized calculation in 30 seconds...`;
changes.after: desc += `. Example calculation for a fixed scenario. For your exact numbers, use the full Mortgage Calculator.`;
changes.rationale: Removed urgency/claim, added parent calculator CTA.

changes.before: let desc = `Find out YOUR exact monthly payment for a`;
changes.after: let desc = `Monthly payment example for a`;

changes.before: desc += `. See amortization schedule... Instant results — 100% free, no signup.`;
changes.after: desc += `. Example payment breakdown for a fixed scenario. For your exact numbers, use the full Loan Calculator.`;
changes.rationale: Replaced benefit claims with example framing + parent CTA.

changes.before: desc += `? See YOUR personalized compound growth projection...`;
changes.after: desc += `? Example growth projection for a fixed scenario. For your exact numbers, use the full Compound Interest Calculator.`;
changes.rationale: Replaced personalization claim with example framing + parent CTA.

Investment-return meta description (removing final value from snippet) was the ONE anti-cannibalization improvement. KEPT as is. This change reduces zero-click snippet cannibalization by not revealing the answer in the meta.

verification: Titles now use descriptive 'Example' language. Meta descriptions point users to parent calculators for custom calculations.
